creator: thefloridaqueenie
caption:
Looking for things to do in Florida? 👀 
This spot (plus 1,000+ more) is in my Florida Travel Guide + Interactive Map perfect for weekend getaways, road trips, and exploring without hours of research and stress! 💬 Comment “GUIDE” and I’ll send it to you!

If fluffy Highland cows are your love language, you’re going to love this place! @cowcreekfarm 

I spent the day at Cow Creek Farm in Plant City, Florida, and it’s one of the sweetest, most wholesome spots you can visit in the area. From baby Highland cows to alpacas, goats, donkeys, and more — it’s a full-on feel-good farm day.

🐄 What You Can Expect:
 • Meet & feed adorable Scottish Highland cows (yes — the fluffy ones!)
 • Interact with goats, alpacas, donkeys & mini horses
 • Seasonal events, photo ops & educational farm tours
 • Family-run charm and tons of opportunities to get up close with the animals
 • Relaxing, peaceful Florida countryside views you didn’t know you needed

Whether you’re planning a fun day trip, looking for kid-friendly activities, or you just want to hug a fluffy cow… this farm is 100% worth the visit.
 Address: 6802 Five Acre Rd, Plant City, FL 33565

🎟️ Ticket Prices: Tickets are around $25–$30 depending on the season and event. Kids are typically a bit less — making this a perfect family or friends outing.

🧾 How to Get Tickets: All tickets are sold online only through their website and social pages. They book up fast, especially on weekends, so grab your spot early!
 Tag the person you’re bringing with you — because once they see these cows, they’re IN. 🐮✨

#scottishhighlandcows #cowcreekfarm #plantcityflorida

transcription.text: Did you know you can feed and pet fluffy Scottish Highland cows right here in Florida? Cow Creek Farm, located in Plant City, Florida, is known for their farm tours where you can feed, brush, and pet the famed Scottish Highland cows. During their tour, you'll meet all their cute farm animals and then take a tractor ride to the back of the farm where you can feed and pet the full-grown Highland cows. The tours have a limited group size, so you'll be sure to have lots of one-on-one time with these cute fluffballs. The tour lasts about an hour, but once the tour is over, you're welcome to stay as long as you like, grab a refreshing lemonade, and hang out with all the other farm animals like the goats, alpacas, miniature donkeys, and chickens. This is the perfect activity for couples, a family outing, or girls day. Send this to someone you want to go with and check out Cow Creek Farm's Instagram page for more details on tickets.
